title: महालक्ष्मी अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री महालक्ष्मी अष्टकम् ॥

इन्द्र उवाच -

नमस्तेऽस्तु महामाये श्रीपीठे सुरपूजिते।
शंखचक्रगदाहस्ते महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१॥

नमस्ते गरुडारूढे कोलासुरभयंकरि।
सर्वपापहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥२॥

सर्वज्ञे सर्ववरदे सर्वदुष्टभयंकरि।
सर्वदुःखहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥३॥

सिद्धिबुद्धिप्रदे देवि भुक्तिमुक्तिप्रदायिनि।
मन्त्रमूर्ते सदा देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥४॥

आद्यन्तरहिते देवि आद्यशक्तिमहेश्वरि।
योगजे योगसम्भूते महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥५॥

स्थूलसूक्ष्ममहारौद्रे महाशक्तिमहोदरे।
महापापहरे देवि महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥६॥

पद्मासनस्थिते देवि परब्रह्मस्वरूपिणि।
परमेशि जगन्मातर्महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥७॥

श्वेताम्बरधरे देवि नानालंकारभूषिते।
जगत्स्थिते जगन्मातर्महालक्ष्मि नमोऽस्तु ते॥८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
महालक्ष्म्यष्टकं स्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिमान्नरः।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति राज्यं प्राप्नोति सर्वदा॥

एककाले पठेन्नित्यं महापापविनाशनम्।
द्विकालं यः पठेन्नित्यं धनधान्यसमन्वितम्॥

त्रिकालं यः पठेन्नित्यं महाशत्रुविनाशनम्।
महालक्ष्मीर्भवेन्नित्यं प्रसन्ना वरदा शुभा॥

॥ इति इन्द्रकृत श्री महालक्ष्मी अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mahalakshmi Ashtakam ||

Indra Uvacha -

Namaste'stu Mahamaye Shripithe Surapujite.
Shankha-chakra-gada-haste Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||1||

Namaste Garudarudhe Kolasura-bhayankari.
Sarva-papa-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||2||

Sarvajne Sarva-varade Sarva-dushta-bhayankari.
Sarva-duhkha-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||3||

Siddhi-buddhi-prade Devi Bhukti-mukti-pradayini.
Mantra-murte Sada Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||4||

Adyanta-rahite Devi Adya-shakti-maheshwari.
Yogaje Yoga-sambhute Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||5||

Sthula-sukshma-maha-raudre Maha-shakti-mahodare.
Maha-papa-hare Devi Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||6||

Padmasana-sthite Devi Parabrahma-swarupini.
Parameshi Jagan-matar Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||7||

Shvetambara-dhare Devi Nanalamkara-bhushite.
Jagat-sthite Jagan-matar Mahalakshmi Namo'stu Te.||8||

|| Phalashruti ||
Mahalakshmyashtakam stotram yah pathed bhaktiman narah.
Sarvasiddhim avapnoti rajyam prapnoti sarvada.

Eka-kale pathen nityam maha-papa-vinashanam.
Dwi-kalam yah pathen nityam dhana-dhanya-samanvitam.

Tri-kalam yah pathen nityam maha-shatru-vinashanam.
Mahalakshmeer bhavet nityam prasanna varada shubha.

|| Iti Indrakrita Shri Mahalakshmi Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
इन्द्र बोले -

हे महामाये! हे श्रीपीठ पर स्थित! हे देवताओं द्वारा पूजित! हे शंख, चक्र और गदा धारण करने वाली! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे गरुड़ पर आरूढ! हे कोलासुर को भय देने वाली! हे समस्त पापों को हरने वाली देवी! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे सर्वज्ञ! हे सबको वरदान देने वाली! हे समस्त दुष्टों को भय देने वाली! हे समस्त दुःखों को हरने वाली देवी! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

हे सिद्धि और बुद्धि देने वाली देवी! हे भोग और मोक्ष प्रदान करने वाली! हे मन्त्रस्वरूपिणी! हे सदा विद्यमान देवी! हे महालक्ष्मी! आपको नमस्कार है।

जो भक्तिमान पुरुष इस महालक्ष्मी अष्टक का पाठ करता है, वह सभी सिद्धियों को प्राप्त करता है और सदैव राज्य प्राप्त करता है। एक समय पाठ से महापाप नष्ट होते हैं, दो समय पाठ से धन-धान्य प्राप्त होता है, तीन समय पाठ से महाशत्रु नष्ट होते हैं।
benefits: Invokes all siddhis (spiritual accomplishments) when recited with devotion
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins when recited once daily
Invokes the blessing of wealth and grains when recited twice daily
Traditionally recited for removal of great enemies when recited three times daily
Traditionally recited to invoke the eternal grace and blessings of mahalakshmi
Invokes both material enjoyment (bhukti) and spiritual liberation (mukti)
description: Mahalakshmi Ashtakam (महालक्ष्मी अष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi. Drawn from Padma Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to grant all siddhis (spiritual accomplishments) when recited with devotion, grant both material enjoyment (bhukti) and spiritual liberation (mukti), and bestow wealth and grains when recited twice daily. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and during Diwali for enhanced blessings. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Mahalakshmi Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mahalakshmi Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight sacred verses by Indra praising Goddess Mahalakshmi for wealth and blessings.
alsoKnownAs: Eight Verses of Mahalakshmi by Indra
relatedSearches: Mahalakshmi Ashtakam lyrics Sanskrit Devanagari Indra
Mahalakshmi Ashtakam benefits eka kale dwi kale tri kale
Mahalakshmi Ashtakam Diwali Lakshmi Puja recitation

seoFaqs.question: What is Mahalakshmi Ashtakam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: Mahalakshmi Ashtakam is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn composed by Indra, king of the devas, in praise of Goddess Mahalakshmi. The composition is preserved in the Padma Purana within the Uttara Khanda section. According to the Puranic narrative, Indra recited these verses after Mahalakshmi rescued the heavenly realm from the demon Kolasura, and she granted him the boon that anyone who recites this stotram with devotion will obtain her grace. The hymn opens with Indra Uvacha — Indra spoke — and then proceeds through eight salutations to the Goddess, concluding with a phalashruti that grants graduated benefits according to the frequency of recitation. (Sources: Padma Purana Uttara Khanda; stotra-nidhi.com Mahalakshmi Ashtakam; vedadhara.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Is the Mahalakshmi Ashtakam the same as the Lakshmi Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes — Mahalakshmi Ashtakam and Lakshmi Ashtakam refer to the same eight-verse Indra-krita hymn from the Padma Purana, with both names used interchangeably in publications and digital sources. The verses are identical from the opening Namastestu Mahamaye through the closing Shvetambara-dhare Devi, and the phalashruti is identical too. The Maha- prefix is added in many traditions to emphasise the supremacy of Lakshmi as the Maha-Devi (Great Goddess) and to distinguish from any local or regional Lakshmi-stotra of the same name. Devotees can confidently use either title; the text and benefits are one. (Sources: Padma Purana editions; comparative Sanskrit stotra collations.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous tri-kala phalashruti of Mahalakshmi Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti specifies a graded reward depending on how many times the stotram is recited daily. Eka-kale pathen nityam maha-papa-vinashanam — recitation once a day destroys great sins. Dwi-kalam yah pathen nityam dhana-dhanya-samanvitam — recitation twice daily grants wealth and abundance of grain. Tri-kalam yah pathen nityam maha-shatru-vinashanam — recitation three times daily destroys mighty enemies. The phalashruti also promises sarva-siddhi (all spiritual perfections) and rajyam (sovereignty/kingship) for any sincere reciter, making the Ashtakam one of the most comprehensive eight-verse hymns in the Lakshmi tradition. (Sources: phalashruti verses 1-3; traditional Lakshmi-vrata commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Indra address Lakshmi as Mahamaya in the opening verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening salutation Namaste'stu Mahamaye Shripithe Surapujite identifies Lakshmi with Maha-Maya, the supreme cosmic illusion-power of the Devi tradition who is also addressed in the Devi Mahatmya. By calling her Mahamaya rather than merely Lakshmi, Indra acknowledges that the Goddess of fortune is not separate from the supreme Shakti who creates, sustains and dissolves the cosmos. Shripithe — seated on the throne of Sri — and Surapujite — worshipped by the devas — establish her as the supreme deity of the celestial realm. This non-dual identification of Lakshmi with the Maha-Devi is one of the distinguishing features of the Padma Purana's Lakshmi theology. (Sources: Padma Purana Lakshmi-mahatmya; Devi Bhagavatam parallels.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the symbolism of Lakshmi mounted on Garuda in the second verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The second verse, Namaste Garudarudhe Kolasura-bhayankari, depicts Lakshmi seated on Garuda — the eagle-mount usually associated with Vishnu — striking terror into the demon Kolasura. This is the warrior-Lakshmi or Vira-Lakshmi aspect, which complements the more familiar Padma-Lakshmi (lotus-seated, peaceful) iconography. By using Vishnu's vahana, the verse asserts the inseparability of the divine couple: when Lakshmi must act in protective fury, she does so with Vishnu's full power at her command. The Kolasura episode is one of several Puranic narratives in which Lakshmi takes a militant role in protecting the cosmic order. (Sources: Padma Purana Lakshmi-mahatmya; iconographic studies of Vira-Lakshmi.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should Mahalakshmi Ashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is the most auspicious weekly day, and the major annual occasions are Diwali (Lakshmi Puja on Kartik Amavasya), Dhanteras, Varalakshmi Vrata (Shravan Shukla Friday before Purnima), Akshaya Tritiya, Margashirsha Vara-Lakshmi Vrata, and Sharad Navratri. The recitation procedure: bathe, wear clean clothes (yellow, red or pink), sit facing east before a Lakshmi murti or yantra, light a ghee lamp, offer kumkum, lotus or rose flowers, sweet naivedya (kheer or ladoo), and recite the eight verses with focused mind. Many Indian businesses recite it at the start of the working day as the cash register is opened. The Diwali night recitation in front of the Lakshmi-Ganesh-Kubera puja is the most popular observance across India. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Diwali; Lakshmi-vrata kalpa traditions.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the spiritual meaning of bhukti-mukti-pradayini in verse 4?
seoFaqs.answer: The compound bhukti-mukti-pradayini in verse 4 (Siddhi-buddhi-prade devi bhukti-mukti-pradayini mantra-murte sada devi) declares Mahalakshmi as the bestower of both bhukti — material enjoyment, prosperity, sensual fulfilment — and mukti — spiritual liberation, freedom from rebirth. This dual capacity is the central theological signature of Mahalakshmi: the bhakti tradition affirms that material and spiritual fulfilment are not opposed, and that the same Divine Mother who gives wealth and worldly success also grants final liberation. The verse therefore makes Mahalakshmi the supreme deity for grihastha (householder) practitioners who seek balance between worldly responsibilities and spiritual progress. (Sources: Sri Vidya Lakshmi tradition; Lakshmi Tantra commentaries.)
